description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, beige stone block with a prominent carved rosette or star-shaped motif on one of its broad faces. The stone has an uneven, weathered surface with visible chips and irregularities, especially along the lower edge, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The sides are relatively flat but not perfectly smooth, with natural textural variations and some angular facets. The back face appears less detailed, with a more polygonal, unadorned surface. The stone is thick and solid, with a mass that would make it difficult to move without assistance. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a historical marker, boundary stone, or decorative urban feature.
description: A beige, weathered stone block featuring a carved rosette motif on one face, with rough, irregular surfaces and a solid, heavy form.
description_view_0: The front face displays a carved rosette or star-shaped motif, with visible surface weathering and chipping along the lower edge.
description_view_1: A narrow side view showing the stone's thickness and the rough, uneven texture of its edge.
description_view_2: The back face is plain and polygonal, lacking any carved details and showing a more angular, faceted surface.
description_view_3: Another narrow side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the stone's depth and rough, natural edge.
attribute_location: urban park boundary or historical plaza
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or boundary marker on the ground
attribute_pattern: Carved rosette or star-shaped motif on the front face, with irregular natural texturing elsewhere.
attribute_weathering: Rough and chipped with visible signs of aging and surface erosion, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or landscaped urban environments, often standing out as a decorative or historical element.
